title: panicum elliottianum
intro: Panicum elliottianum, also known as Elliott's panic grass, is a type of grass native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
